content.title: Bringing live chat to Sydney Health BCBS
content.summary: I led the end-to-end design of a live chat feature for Sydney Health's mobile app — from initial research through shipping — resulting in 50K+ interactions, 35% reduction in churn, 40% accessibility improvement, and 25% fewer complaints within six months.

content.body: Before this project, members had one option when they needed help: call customer service and wait. Average hold times were 12 minutes. Claims were confusing, physician access felt impossible, and members were leaving. Customer service was costing Sydney Health $2.5M a year and churn was running 35% above industry average. The business needed a way to give members immediate, secure support without adding headcount.

I ran observations across app usage and CS call logs, conducted 25 interviews spanning both younger and elderly members, and surveyed 200 users. The clearest signal: 78% of members wanted real-time support but weren't getting it. Phone wait times and claim confusion came up in nearly every interview. Elderly users specifically struggled with the existing interface — font sizes, tap targets, and navigation were all working against them.

The biggest shift was moving from a phone-first mental model to a chat-first one. The old flow forced members to leave the app entirely to get help. I designed an in-app chat surface that surfaced contextually — so if you were looking at a claim, the chat already knew that. Average response time dropped from 12 minutes to under 2.

The second major decision was personalization over generic flows. Rather than starting every conversation from scratch, the chat pre-populated member data from their profile and backend records. This alone cut resolution time by 25% — members weren't repeating themselves to every agent.

The third decision was treating security as a design problem, not a compliance checkbox. Consent flows, biometric auth, and encryption indicators were designed to be visible and understandable — not buried in settings. Trust scores went up 32% and the feature passed third-party HIPAA review without changes.

HIPAA compliance meant every design decision needed an audit trail and end-to-end encryption from day one — I worked directly with the security team to prototype compliant flows before any visual design was done. Elderly usability was the hardest problem — I added voice input and large touch targets after Maze testing showed a 45% improvement in task success. Stakeholder alignment took three full Figma critique sessions before we reached a version everyone could ship.

Pre-populated data matters more than features — members don't want more options, they want less friction. Making security visible rather than invisible actually builds trust instead of eroding it. And testing on mobile-first with real thumb zones early caught 70% of interaction issues before they ever reached engineering.

content.text:
Accessibility: Prominent iOS/Android chat for plans/claims/ID/physician consults.

Personalization: Profile/backend integration for real-time data, secure messaging/video.

Privacy: E2E encryption, biometrics, consent protocols.
